query: Strait of Hormuz ship transit status June 2026, Iran blockade, current vessel traffic levels
answer:
This is an extraordinarily comprehensive dataset. Let me compile the final report.

---

# Strait of Hormuz Ship Transit Status — June 2026

## Executive Summary

**The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed to commercial shipping since February 28, 2026** — approximately 108 days as of mid-June 2026. This is the most severe disruption to maritime energy transit in modern history, triggered by the US-Israeli "Operation Midnight Hammer" strikes on Iran and Iran's retaliatory closure. A diplomatic deal framework exists with a signing ceremony scheduled for June 19, 2026, but physical reopening remains highly uncertain.

---

## 1. Current Ship Transit Status

- **Commercial vessel traffic: effectively zero** since late February 2026.
- Tanker traffic dropped ~70% immediately after closure, then fell to near zero.
- On **June 10–11, 2026**, the IRGC formally declared **"complete closure"** and published expanded boundary maps extending into UAE territorial waters.
- **~2,000 ships and ~20,000 mariners from 87 countries remain stranded.**
- S&P Global described it as **"the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy."**

### Pre-War Baseline
- Normal throughput: **~20.5 million barrels/day** of crude oil and petroleum products (EIA).
- Represents ~25% of world seaborne oil and ~20% of global LNG.

---

## 2. Iran Blockade — Active, Not Theoretical

Iran's closure is not a threat but an **ongoing, active blockade** since February 28, 2026.

| Date | Action |
|------|--------|
| **Feb 28** | Iran closed the Strait in retaliation for Operation Midnight Hammer (11,000+ targets struck in Iran; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed) |
| **May 4–5** | Iran attacked UAE Fujairah oil facilities (12 ballistic missiles, 3 cruise missiles, 4 drones) |
| **Jun 10–11** | IRGC declared "complete closure," extended boundary claims into UAE waters |

---

## 3. Military Situation

- **Three US Carrier Strike Groups** deployed under "Project Freedom" naval escort (since May 4).
- Active strike exchanges continued through June 10–11 (US struck Bandar Abbas/Qeshm Island; Iran shot down US Apache helicopter).
- **US casualties**: 15 killed, 543 wounded. **Iranian casualties**: 3,468 killed.
- Trump announced a 60-day ceasefire extension on June 11.

---

## 4. Oil Price & Economic Impact

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Brent crude (pre-war, Feb 27) | ~$72.50/bbl |
| Brent crude (peak, late April) | $126.10/bbl |
| Brent crude (Jun 15) | $83.63/bbl |
| US gasoline (pre-war) | $2.98/gal |
| US gasoline (peak, May 3) | $4.45/gal (+49%) |
| Mediterranean diesel crack spread | $62–63/bbl (3–6× normal) |
| Net global oil supply loss | ~2 million bpd (IEA) |
| US war cost | ~$27 billion through May 2026 |
| Iran daily blockade losses | ~$435 million/day |

---

## 5. Insurance & Shipping Rates

- Insurance companies remain **"largely unwilling"** to cover Hormuz transit, even under military escort.
- **Fitch assessed broad Hormuz reopening as unlikely before July 2026.**
- Brent crude annualized volatility at 41.5% (exceptionally elevated).

---

## 6. Deal Status & Reopening Prospects

- **June 14**: Trump announced deal "complete"; authorized removal of naval blockade. Iran's Deputy FM confirmed. MOU reportedly digitally signed.
- **June 19 (scheduled)**: Formal signing at Burgenstock Resort, Switzerland.
- **Deal terms**: Strait to reopen immediately without tolls; full restoration within ~30 days; 60-day ceasefire.

### Key Risks to Reopening
1. **IRGC has NOT endorsed the deal** — yet it operationally controls the closure.
2. Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not made a verified public appearance in 99 days.
3. Israeli opposition (Netanyahu struck Beirut on deal announcement day).
4. No commercial insurance coverage available for transit.
5. Mine-clearing operations required before commercial traffic can resume.

### Prediction Markets (Jun 15)
| Question | Probability |
|----------|-------------|
| US-Iran formally sign agreement before Jun 26 | **75%** (Metaculus) |
| Iran reopen Strait before Jun 27 | **40%** (Metaculus) |
| US-Iran sign by Jul 31 | **~81.5%** (Polymarket) |

The **35-point gap** between signing probability (75%) and reopening probability (40%) reflects deep market skepticism that a signed deal will translate to actual physical reopening.

---

## 7. Supply Offsets

- US SPR release: 17.5 million barrels (Mar–Apr 2026)
- OPEC+ increase: only 188,000 bpd starting June (far below 2M bpd loss)
- Venezuela exports surged to 1.23 million bpd (7-year high)
- UAE departed OPEC during the crisis

---
